skill.name: kling-3-0
skill.description: Use RunComfy to generate Kling 3.0 videos across Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers. The skill helps choose text-to-video or image-to-video endpoints, shape prompts, set duration and aspect ratio, and invoke the local RunComfy CLI.
skill.summary: Generate Kling 3.0 text-to-video and image-to-video clips through RunComfy.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are Markdown examples, endpoint names, placeholder URLs, or documentation text rather than executable malicious code. The remaining confirmed risk is operational: the skill instructs use of the local RunComfy CLI, sends prompts and image URLs to a third-party API, handles tokens, and downloads generated files.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users or agents to install and run the RunComfy CLI. This is legitimate for the advertised function, but it executes a local third-party command and should be trusted before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and workflow clearly invoke runcomfy, but they are documented CLI calls for the stated video generation purpose. No evidence found of hidden shell injection or unrelated command execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Submission and Downloads
medium_findings.description: Prompts, public image URLs, and generated outputs are handled through RunComfy services. This creates privacy and data sharing risk for sensitive prompts, proprietary images, or confidential media requests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly says the CLI posts to the RunComfy Model API and downloads from RunComfy domains. This is core product behavior, not evidence of malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Token Storage and Environment Token Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents storing a RunComfy token in the user configuration directory or passing it through RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. Token exposure would allow use of the user account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token paths and environment variable are documented directly. The file mode guidance lowers risk, but credentials remain sensitive.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code, tables, and fenced command examples. They are not executable code inside this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown documentation with no script file or runtime wrapper. The flagged backticks are formatting and example commands.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts correspond to placeholder URL ellipses in image examples and output directory placeholders. No evidence found of filesystem traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged sequences appear inside illustrative values such as HTTPS placeholders, not code that reads arbitrary paths. The skill states local image files are not supported.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic operations. The cited lines describe model names, prompts, audio settings, and CLI exit code documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No hashing, encryption, signing, or security-sensitive cryptographic code appears in the reviewed Markdown. The detections are semantic false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Third-Party CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on runcomfy run commands to submit generation jobs. Users should review the installed CLI and avoid running it with untrusted shell modifications.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI invocation is explicit and central to the skill. The examples pass JSON as a quoted argument, which limits direct shell expansion risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Content Download to Output Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated media is downloaded from RunComfy domains into a local output directory. Large generated files can consume storage, although the skill documents a per-file size cap.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The download behavior and size cap are documented. This is expected behavior, but it still affects local filesystem storage.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable way to choose the right Kling 3.0 endpoint and prepare valid generation inputs. This skill guides RunComfy CLI calls for text-to-video, image-to-video, audio, tiers, and output settings.
actual_capabilities: Selects among Standard, Pro, and 4K Kling 3.0 tiers based on quality and budget needs.
Routes requests to text-to-video or image-to-video endpoints.
Documents supported input fields such as prompt, duration, aspect ratio, seed, and audio.
Provides RunComfy CLI invocation patterns for all six Kling 3.0 endpoints.
Offers prompt guidance for multi-shot scenes, identity anchors, camera movement, and audio.
Explains pricing tradeoffs, duration caps, aspect ratios, and output handling.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and either login or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN.
Image-to-video requires publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs, not local image files.
Each generation is limited to the documented duration and aspect ratio options.
Sensitive prompts or source images are sent to RunComfy services.

use_cases.title: Prototype Social Video Variants
use_cases.description: Use the Standard tier to test short vertical or square clips before spending on higher fidelity output.

use_cases.title: Create Brand Hero Clips
use_cases.description: Use the Pro or 4K tier for polished campaign videos with stronger motion and identity consistency.

use_cases.title: Animate Product or Character Images
use_cases.description: Use image-to-video prompts to preserve a reference image while adding controlled camera motion and ambience.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5 second Kling 3.0 Standard text-to-video draft in 16:9. Scene: a calm product reveal on a clean studio table.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Kling 3.0 image-to-video with this HTTPS image URL. Preserve the subject, add a slow camera push, and keep motion natural.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Shot Ad
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an 8 second Kling V3.0 Pro clip with three shots, consistent character identity, synchronized ambience, and a clear product moment.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a 4K Master
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Kling V3.0 4K for a 10 second cinematic master. Include native audio, 16:9 framing, and production-ready camera direction.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a fast 9:16 cafe video for social testing.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends the Standard text-to-video endpoint, a 5 second duration, vertical aspect ratio, and a concise motion-focused prompt.

output_examples.input: A user provides a product image URL and wants a premium launch animation.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends Pro or 4K image-to-video, restates identity preservation instructions, and includes audio guidance when needed.

output_examples.input: A user wants one multi-shot brand scene with dialogue and ambience.
output_examples.output: The skill structures numbered shots, enables synchronized audio, and warns about cost before selecting a higher tier.

best_practices: Start with Standard tier drafts before using Pro or 4K for final output.
Use clear camera, motion, subject, and audio instructions in the prompt.
Avoid sending confidential prompts, private images, or restricted client assets to third-party services.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for clips beyond the supported per-call duration limit.
Do not combine conflicting style, camera, and subject instructions in one prompt.
Do not use public image URLs that expose private or licensed material without permission.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate Kling 3.0 videos through RunComfy using text-to-video or image-to-video endpoints.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the RunComfy CLI and authentication through login or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN.

faq.question: Can it use local image files?
faq.answer: No. The skill documents image-to-video inputs as publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs.

faq.question: Which tier should I use first?
faq.answer: Use Standard for drafts, Pro for high quality 1080p, and 4K only when native 4K output is needed.

faq.question: Does it support audio?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented generate_audio option enables synchronized audio, with different pricing by tier.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive media?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and image URLs are submitted to RunComfy services, so avoid confidential assets.
